overallSummary: Fabio Giglietto maintains an active presence across Mastodon, LinkedIn, and Bluesky, focusing on research related to computational social science, disinformation, and information operations. His LinkedIn profile boasts the largest follower count, indicating a strong professional network. Engagement varies across platforms, suggesting tailored content strategies may be beneficial.

contentThemes.description: Posts related to research findings, methodologies, and data science applications in social media analysis.
Content focusing on detecting, analyzing, and understanding coordinated inauthentic behavior and information manipulation tactics.
Posts related to hiring postdoctoral researchers and recruiting PhD students for research projects.
Announcements of conference presentations and publications related to ongoing research efforts.

recommendations: Tailor content for each platform to maximize engagement. LinkedIn benefits from professional announcements and job postings, while Bluesky and Mastodon may resonate more with research updates and discussions.
Encourage more interaction by posing questions and prompting responses in posts.
Use relevant hashtags consistently to improve discoverability.

platformInsights.bluesky: Bluesky sees consistent engagement with research-related announcements and opportunities. Reposts are a significant factor here.
platformInsights.mastodon: Mastodon posts see moderate engagement, focusing on released tools and methodological discussions.
platformInsights.linkedin: LinkedIn generates the highest engagement, especially for posts announcing grants and job opportunities, indicating a professional audience.

topPerformingContent.content: Excited to announce that our research team has been awarded a new grant to study information operations across social media platforms.

Now hiring: Two postdoctoral researchers to join our team working on computational methods for social media analysis. Background in data science, computational social science, or related fields preferred.
Just published our new paper on coordinated inauthentic behavior on social media platforms!
